observation.summary: Executable discovery and child process PATH behavior were described as prose while proof lived across detectutil and runtime spawn tests.

hypothesis.summary: A manifest can generate the reader doc and prove search order, override fail-closed behavior, and launch PATH propagation from executable behavior plus source checks.

execution.summary: Run executablesearchpath to check generated docs, source references, temp executable path ordering, override pinning, and spawn PATH propagation.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects generated doc drift, source drift, PATH order drift, override fallback regressions, and child process PATH propagation regressions.

retrospective.summary: Executable search path behavior becomes executable local-runtime evidence instead of a manually edited config note.

evidence.proves: executable search path manifest matches generated docs, source references, and PATH behavior
public CI uploads executable search path evidence
